name: Ubuntu
description: A popular beginner-friendly distribution with strong hardware support, regular releases, and a large community.

name: Kubuntu
description: Ubuntu with the KDE Plasma desktop, offering a modern and highly customizable interface.

name: Xubuntu
description: Ubuntu with the lightweight Xfce desktop, suitable for older hardware and users who want a fast system.

name: Lubuntu
description: An Ubuntu flavour built around the lightweight LXQt desktop for fast performance on low-resource systems.

name: Ubuntu MATE
description: Ubuntu with the MATE desktop environment, offering a classic desktop layout with modern polish.

name: Ubuntu Budgie
description: Ubuntu with the Budgie desktop, focusing on simplicity, elegance, and a modern user experience.

name: Ubuntu Studio
description: A multimedia-focused Ubuntu flavour with a collection of audio, video, and graphics creation tools.

name: Linux Mint
description: A polished Ubuntu-based distro with a traditional desktop experience and a strong focus on ease of use.

name: Fedora
description: A cutting-edge distribution backed by Red Hat, ideal for users who want the latest open-source software.

name: Pop!_OS
description: A developer-friendly Ubuntu derivative with strong support for gaming and productivity workflows.

name: Manjaro
description: An Arch-based distribution that simplifies installation and maintenance while offering access to a rolling release model.
desktop: Multiple (GNOME, KDE Plasma, Xfce) (other desktops available through community editions)

name: Arch Linux
description: A minimalist, highly customizable rolling-release distribution for advanced users who prefer to build their system from scratch.
desktop: Multiple (GNOME, KDE Plasma, Xfce, MATE, LXQt, Budgie, i3, sway, and more)

name: Debian
description: A stable and reliable distribution valued for its long release cycle and extensive package repository.
desktop: Multiple (GNOME, KDE Plasma, Xfce, MATE, LXQt, Budgie, and more)

name: elementary OS
description: A design-focused distribution with a polished macOS-like desktop experience and curated default apps.

name: Zorin OS
description: A user-friendly distribution designed to be familiar for users switching from Windows or macOS.

name: Bazzzite
description: A community-driven Linux distribution focused on modularity, performance, and visual customization.

name: CachyOS
description: A performance-focused Ubuntu derivative with optimized packages and a polished KDE experience.

name: EndeavourOS
description: An Arch-based distribution that offers a user-friendly installer and access to Arch repositories.
desktop: Multiple (GNOME, KDE Plasma, Xfce, and more)

name: Gentoo Linux
description: A source-based distribution built around customization and performance tuning.

name: KDE Neon
description: A KDE-focused distro based on Ubuntu LTS, offering the latest Plasma desktop and KDE apps.

name: Nix OS
description: A declarative distribution with reproducible system configuration using the Nix package manager.

name: Nobara Linux
description: A Fedora-based distribution tuned for gaming and multimedia content creation.

name: openSUSE
description: A flexible distribution offering Leap for stability and Tumbleweed for rolling releases.
desktop: Multiple (GNOME, KDE Plasma, Xfce, and more)
